text.title: Speech-to-Text Basics Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for production Whisper-based transcription.

rule.statement: Pin language= explicitly (BCP-47 or 2-letter). Auto-detect adds ~200ms and misclassifies similar languages (UK↔RU, SR↔HR).
rule.rationale: Wrong language → transliterated nonsense or full mis-translation.

rule.statement: Use response_format="verbose_json" + timestamp_granularities=["segment"] for production transcripts.
rule.rationale: Plain-text response loses segment boundaries needed for downstream alignment / search.

rule.statement: Accept only Whisper-supported formats: MP3, MP4, MPEG, MPGA, M4A, WAV, WEBM. Reject FLAC and OGG at the edge.
rule.rationale: Sending unsupported formats fails late in the pipeline; rejecting at ingest fails fast.

rule.statement: Pre-split files larger than 25 MB with pydub (or ffmpeg) on silence boundaries. Concatenate transcripts in order.
rule.rationale: 25 MB is a hard API cap; splitting on silence avoids cutting words mid-sentence.

rule.statement: Above ~10000 audio hours/month, switch to local faster-whisper on GPU. The API price curve breaks above that point.
rule.rationale: API at $0.006/min is cheap but adds up at scale; local faster-whisper amortises hardware.

rule.statement: Whisper is batch-only. Sub-300ms streaming requires Deepgram / AssemblyAI / Azure Realtime.
rule.rationale: Building "streaming with Whisper" by hand stitches end-of-segment results and never beats native streaming.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire Whisper transcription per procedure.
Real-time streaming OR unsupported format OR very noisy audio → skip.
